seoTitle: Williamstown vs Wilson — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Williamstown and Wilson. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Williamstown and Wilson.
heading: Williamstown vs Wilson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Williamstown, Western Australia and Wilson, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Williamstown and Wilson have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Williamstown or Wilson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Williamstown has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Wilson scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Williamstown scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Wilson scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Williamstown or Wilson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Williamstown has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
